scope_summary: State Act establishing the Heritage Council of Western Australia and the State Register of Heritage Places, replacing the Heritage of Western Australia Act 1990. It regulates entry, amendment and removal of register entries, protection orders, repair orders and stop work orders, and requires decision-makers (including planning authorities) to refer development proposals affecting registered places to the Council for advice before deciding them (Part 5). It also provides for heritage agreements, local heritage surveys by local governments (Part 8), conservation assistance and modification of planning instruments, applying statewide wherever heritage places exist.
